headline: LinkedIn data shows AI isn't to blame for hiring decline… yet
angle: The dark comedy of LinkedIn's defensive preemptive absolution—they're clearing AI of charges it hasn't even been formally accused of yet, which is exactly what someone says right before they get caught.
scene: A corporate HR executive in a sterile office, sweating slightly, holding a press release. A chart on the wall behind him shows hiring declining sharply. LinkedIn's logo looms ominously in the background.
setup: LinkedIn announces AI isn't responsible for 20% hiring decline
punchline: We'd like to clear that up before you ask. Also, we're raising prices.
instagram_caption: LinkedIn rushing to exonerate AI for mass layoffs it hasn't caused yet. Very normal behavior.
image_prompt: Single-panel cartoon, clean line art, thick black outlines, muted blue-gray pastel colors, white background, New Yorker magazine aesthetic, flat colors, no shading, no gradients, no signature. A middle-aged man in business casual attire standing at a podium in a corporate conference room, nervously gripping the sides. Behind him, a large bar chart showing a sharp downward slope labeled 'Hiring 2022-2026.' To his left, a LinkedIn logo on the wall. He has a forced smile and visible tension in his shoulders. His tie is slightly askew. A few empty chairs visible in the background of the conference room, suggesting the irony of empty seats.
